beats.narration: Every magnetization job is two questions. Which way must the flux run, and what current arrangement puts it there.

payload.title: TWO DIRECTIONS
payload.sub: Which way the flux runs — and how current gets it there.

beats.narration: Flux must cross a flaw, best between forty-five and ninety degrees. Parallel, it finds nothing. So full coverage means two shots, roughly perpendicular.

payload.heading: The direction rule, always
payload.note: CROSS THE FLAW · 45–90° · TWO SHOTS, ~90° APART

beats.narration: Circular magnetization drives current through the part. Flux circles it, finding flaws parallel to the current — seams along a shaft.

tiles.s: Current end to end, surface field

beats.narration: Longitudinal magnetization sends flux down the axis instead. Place the part in a coil, and it crosses transverse cracks — the ones that grow across a shaft.

payload.heading: Longitudinal: flux down the axis
payload.note: COIL FLUX RUNS LENGTHWISE → FINDS TRANSVERSE CRACKS

beats.narration: Lock the craft in three steps. Two directions for coverage, calculated current to drive them, a verified field to prove the shot.

payload.sub: Calculate the current, then prove it with a shim and gaussmeter.
